THE COURT DETERMINES THAT THERE IS GOOD CAUSE TO WARRANT SEALING PURSUANT TO CAL. RULE OF COURT 2.550. CROSS-DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AN ORDER LISTING THE PRECISE DOCUMENTS TO BE SEALED AND SET FORTH THE FOLLOWING FINDINGS: (1) THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE RECORD; (2) THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THE RECORD; (3) A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; (4) THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND (5) NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. Notice Of Unopposed Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Mar-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
CROSS-DEFENDANT ALTA FUNDAMENTAL ADVISERS SP LLC- SERIES M, CADBURY HEDGE FUND ALTERNATIVES, AND CANARY SC MASTER FUND, L.P.'s Unopposed Motion To Seal.

Matter called. No argument is held on the unopposed motion to seal. The tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Cross-defendants' unopposed motion to seal exhibits A-C attached to the Agre declaration is granted. The Court determines that there is good cause to warrant sealing pursuant to Cal. Rule of Court 2.550. The adduced documents contain commercially sensitive material.

Cross-defendants shall prepare an order listing the precise documents to be sealed and set forth the following findings: (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Pending Appeal Of Remand Ruling
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK SECURITIES, INC.'S Motion To Stay Pending Appeal Of Remand Ruling.

Defendant Deutsche Bank Securities Inc.'s motion to stay pending its appeal of an order remanding this action from the United States Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of New York to this Court is granted. It would promote judicial economy to stay the action until it can be determined whether the action will be heard in the Southern District of New York, pursuant to the order of the Judicial Panel on Multidistrict Litigation that it be coordinated or consolidated with the ongoing multidistrict litigation relating to the bankruptcy of SunEdison, Inc., or whether it will be heard in this Court. The Court understands that Deutsche Bank is already a defendant in the SunEdison MDL proceedings. The Court further understands that the parties have reached an agreement pursuant to which discovery in this action would be stayed until the earlier of December 31, 2019 or a decision on Deutsche Bank's appeal. (Opp. at 1.) A limited stay pending the disposition of Deutsche Bank's appeal would prevent potentially inconsistent pretrial rulings, and would conserve the Court's and the parties' resources. Plaintiff does not identify any concrete prejudice it would suffer as a result of the requested stay. (See Freiberg v. City of Mission Viejo (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1484, 1489 ["Trial courts generally have the inherent power to stay proceedings in the interests of justice and to promote judicial efficiency."].)

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in complaince with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
